tool.name: seqwish
tool.description: induce a variation graph from PAF alignments
tool.command: seqwish --paf-alns='$paf_alns' --seqs=${ ','.join(["'%s'" % s for s in $seqs]) } --gfa='$out_gfa' #if $match_list --match-list='$match_list' #end if #if str($repeat_max).strip() --repeat-max=$repeat_max #end if --min-match-len=$min_match_len #if str($transclose_batch).strip() --transclose-batch=$transclose_batch #end if --threads=\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}

param.label: PAF alignments
param.help: All-vs-all PAF mappings (typically from wfmash). The graph is induced from these.

param.label: Source sequences
param.help: The FASTA(s)/FASTQ(s) referenced in the PAF query/target names.

param.label: Subset PAF list (-m)
param.help: Restrict transitive closure to the alignments listed in this file.

param.label: Max copies per input base (-r, blank = unlimited)
param.help: Cap the transitive closure at N copies of any input base. Tames runaway repeats.

param.label: Min match length (-k)
param.help: Filter exact matches below this length before induction. 23 is the pggb default; lower values give a finer-grained graph at the cost of complexity.

param.label: Transitive-closure batch size (bp) (-B, blank = default)
param.help: Number of base pairs per transitive-closure batch. Higher = more memory, faster.

tool.help:
**seqwish** — induce a variation graph from pairwise alignments.
Reads a PAF of all-vs-all mappings + the source FASTAs and emits a GFA1 variation graph. The graph is *induced* by the transitive closure of the alignments — every match collapses into a single graph node.
Standard pangenome pipeline step between `wfmash` (mapping) and `smoothxg` (POA-aware smoothing).
-----
**Inputs**
- `PAF alignments` — typically from wfmash all-vs-all. - `Source sequences` — one or more FASTAs whose names appear in the PAF.
**Key parameters**
- `--min-match-len` (`-k`): default 23 in pggb. Smaller values give a finer-grained graph; larger values collapse more locally-divergent regions. - `--repeat-max` (`-r`): caps transitive closure copies; safety against repeat-driven explosions. - `--transclose-batch` (`-B`, bp): memory/time tradeoff.
**Output**
- GFA1 graph; pass to `smoothxg` (then `gfaffix`) for the standard pggb pipeline.
